channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mandatory Cyber Security Oversight Needed for All Health Data Handlers in New Zealand
item.description: Following a spate of serious cyber security breaches affecting health data in New Zealand, the government has outlined a national cyber security strategy covering critical infrastructure, including he...

item.title: CAN President Urges Christians to Promote Peace and Unity Amid Challenges
item.description: As Nigerians dey face wahala with economy and wahala wey dey globally, Archbishop Daniel Okoh, wey be President of the Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), don call on Christians to maintain calm, ...

item.title: Tanzania expands road and bridge network with 79 major projects worth 6 trillion shillings
item.description: Dodoma: Tanzania Roads Agency (TANROADS) is pushing ahead with a major infrastructure push, currently executing 79 big road and bridge projects across the country valued at 6 trillion Tanzanian shilli...

item.title: Northern Governors Promise Support for Tinubu on Security and Economic Reforms
item.description: On Monday, the governors of the 19 northern states under the Northern States Governors’ Forum (NSGF) show clear support for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. Dem meet for Gombe State Governor’s Lodge, Asok...

item.title: Wallabies to face Ireland in Canberra for first time in a decade ahead of 2027 Rugby World Cup
item.description: The Wallabies are set to return to Canberra Stadium for the first time in ten years, taking on world number three Ireland in a major warm-up clash ahead of the 2027 Rugby World Cup. The match, locked ...

item.title: Opposition challenges government focus on Left-wing extremism, cites unemployment and tribal concerns
item.description: In a session of the Lok Sabha on Monday, the opposition criticised the ruling BJP for prioritising a debate on Left-wing extremism (LWE) while other pressing issues were overlooked. Opposition members...

item.title: Impact of Iran Conflict on Gold Market Trends
item.description: The news article discusses the recent developments in the gold market as influenced by the ongoing tensions and conflict in Iran. Geopolitical unrest has historically had a direct impact on commoditie...

item.title: Police Investigate Fatal Stabbing of 16-Year-Old Chloe Watson Dransfield in Leeds
item.description: The family of 16-year-old Chloe Watson Dransfield have paid heartfelt tributes following her tragic death after being stabbed in Leeds. Chloe was found unconscious with serious injuries on Kennerleigh...

item.title: Israel temporarily restricts access to Jerusalem's holy sites during Palm Sunday, later reversed
item.description: During Palm Sunday, Israeli authorities initially prevented Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa and Friar Francesco Ielpo from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem, citing security conc...

item.title: Prince Andrew’s Second-hand Caravan Highlights the Rise of Luxury Mobile Homes in the UK
item.description: The article explores how modern mobile homes have evolved into luxurious and stylish spaces, attracting not only everyday buyers but also members of the British royal family. It reports that Andrew Mo...

item.title: Iran warns US students in Middle East following missile strikes on educational facilities
item.description: Iran has issued a direct warning to American students studying in the Middle East following a series of US-Israeli missile strikes on Iranian schools. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard has demanded that...

item.title: South Africa’s Left Must Stand Firm Against Xenophobia and Right-Wing Politics
item.description: In his opinion piece, Dr Imraan Buccus argues that South Africa’s leftist movements must take a clear, uncompromising stance against the surge of xenophobia being normalised by several political parti...

item.title: Tanzania promotes AI for regional development in agriculture, health, and trade
item.description: During the 4th EAC Regional Science, Technology, and Innovation Conference held in Kigali, Rwanda, Tanzania highlighted the important role of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in driving development across...

item.title: PNP deploys over 11,000 personnel for Holy Week security in Calabarzon and Mimaropa
item.description: Around 11,100 police personnel, together with other force multipliers, are set to maintain safety and order in the Calabarzon and Mimaropa regions during the Holy Week. The Philippine National Police ...

item.title: In parts of Assam, election candidates uphold courtesy despite heated campaigning
item.description: The 2026 Assam Assembly elections have witnessed several constituencies where political competition remains grounded in respect, even as the broader campaign across the State has often turned aggressi...

item.title: Deputy Speaker Kalu Condemns Deadly Attack in Plateau, Urges Action to Protect Lives
item.description: The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, don sharply condemn the recent attack wey happen for Angwan Rukuba inside Jos North Local Government Area. The attack, wey take place...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Outlines Austerity and Relief Measures Amid Global Fuel Crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has assured that the government will continue efforts to extend financial relief to Pakistan’s poor and middle-income citizens despite the ongoing global fuel crisis. Dur...

item.title: Telposta Pension Scheme to Sell Properties to Improve Pension Liquidity
item.description: The Telposta Pension Scheme (TPS) has set in motion a plan to sell part of its extensive property portfolio in order to improve cash flow and ensure timely pension payments to its members. Currently, ...

item.title: NBC hosts forum to strengthen women entrepreneurship in Dar es Salaam
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: National Bank of Commerce (NBC) recently organized a powerful forum for women traders and entrepreneurs in Dar es Salaam as part of its 'Women’s Month' activities celebrating Internatio...

item.title: Lahore Qalandars fines Shaheen Afridi Rs1m for hotel security misunderstanding
item.description: Lahore Qalandars, a Pakistan Super League franchise, has imposed a voluntary fine of Rs1 million on their captain, fast bowler Shaheen Shah Afridi, following an incident of alleged security protocol b...
